title: rubus gracilis subsp. insularis
intro: Rubus gracilis subsp. insularis, also known as the Hawaiian black raspberry, is a type of bramble plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and have anti-cancer properties. Rubus gracilis subsp. insularis can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
